author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …Let this singer ascend; and let this man sing from the heart of each of you, and let each of you be this man, for when each of you saith this, since ye are all one in Christ, one man saith this; and saith not, “Unto Thee, O Lord, have” we “lift up” our “eyes;” but, “Unto Thee, O Lord, have I lift up mine eyes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye ought indeed to imagine that every one of you is speaking; but that One in an especial sense speaketh, who is also spread abroad over the whole world…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What maketh the heart of a Christian heavy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he is a pilgrim, and longeth for his country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thy heart be heavy on this score, although thou hast been prosperous in the world, still thou dost groan: and if all things combine to render thee prosperous, and this world smile upon thee on every side, thou nevertheless groanest, because thou seest that thou art set in a pilgrimage; and feelest that thou hast indeed happiness in the eyes of fools, but not as yet after the promise of Christ: this thou seekest with groans, this thou seekest with longings, and by longing ascendest, and while thou ascendest dost sing the Song of Degrees.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2. …Where then are the ladders?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we behold so great an interval between heaven and earth, there is so wide a separation, and so great a space of regions between: we wish to climb thither, we see no ladder; do we deceive ourselves, because we sing the Song of Degrees, that is, the Song of ascent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We ascend unto heaven, if we think of God, who hath made ascending steps in the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to ascend in heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To advance towards God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As every man who faileth, doth not descend, but falleth: so every one who profiteth doth ascend: but if he so profit, as to avoid pride: if he so ascend as not to fall: but if while he profiteth he become proud, in ascending he again falleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that he may not be proud, what ought he to do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him lift up his eyes unto Him who dwelleth in heaven, let him not heed himself…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If, my brethren, we understand by heaven the firmament which we see with our bodily eyes, we shall indeed so err, as to imagine that we cannot ascend thither without ladders, or some scaling machines: but if we ascend spiritually, we ought to understand heaven spiritually: if the ascent be in affection, heaven is in righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then the heaven of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All holy souls, all righteous souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostles also, although they were on earth in the flesh, were heaven; for the Lord, enthroned in them, traversed the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then dwelleth in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How?…How long are they the temple according to faith?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As long as Christ dwelleth in them through faith; as the Apostle saith, “That Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they are already heaven in whom God already dwelleth visibly, who see Him face to face; all the holy Apostles, all the holy Virtues, Powers, Thrones, Lordships, that heavenly Jerusalem, wanderers from whence we groan, and for which we pray with longing; and there God dwelleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thither hath the Psalmist lifted up his faith, thither he riseth in affection, with longing hopes: and this very longing causeth the soul to purge off the filth of sins, and to be cleansed from every stain, that itself also may become heaven; because it hath lifted up its eyes unto Him who dwelleth in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if we have determined that that heaven which we see with our bodily eyes is the dwelling of God, the dwelling of God will pass away; for “heaven and earth will pass away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then, before God created heaven and earth, where did He dwell?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But some one saith: and before God made the Saints, where did He dwell?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God dwelt in Himself, he dwelt with Himself, and God is with Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when He deigneth to dwell in the Saints, the Saints are not the house of God in such wise, as that God should fall when it is withdrawn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we dwell in a house in one way, in another way God dwelleth in the Saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dwellest in a house: if it be withdrawn, thou fallest: but God so dwelleth in the Saints, that if He should Himself depart, they fall…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then followeth, since he hath said, “Unto Thee do I lift up mine eyes”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How hast thou lifted up thine eyes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, even as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress: even so our eyes wait upon the Lord our God, until He have mercy upon us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are both servants, and a handmaiden: He is both our Master and our Mistress.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do these words mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do these similitudes mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not wonderful if we are servants, and He our Master; but it is wonderful if we are a maiden, and He our Mistress.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But not even our being a maiden is wonderful; for we are the Church: nor is it wonderful that He is our Mistress; for He is the Power and the Wisdom of God…When therefore thou hearest Christ, lift up thine eyes to the hands of thy Master; when thou hearest the Power of God and the Wisdom of God, lift up thine eyes to the hands of thy Mistress; for thou art both servant and handmaiden; servant, for thou art a people; handmaiden, for thou art the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this maiden hath found great dignity with God; she hath been made a wife.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But until she come unto those spiritual embraces, where she may without apprehension enjoy Him whom she hath loved, and for whom she hath sighed in this tedious pilgrimage, she is betrothed: and hath received a mighty pledge, the blood of the Spouse for whom she sigheth without fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor is it said unto her, Do not love; as it is sometimes said to any betrothed virgin, not as yet married: and is justly said, Do not love; when thou hast become a wife, then love: it is rightly said, because it is a precipitate and preposterous thing, and not a chaste desire, to love one whom she knoweth not whether she shall marry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it may happen that one man may be betrothed to her, and another man marry her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as there is no one else who can be preferred to Christ, let her love without apprehension: and before she is joined unto Him, let her love, and sigh from a distance and from her far pilgrimage…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For we have been much filled with contempt” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that will live piously according to Christ, must needs suffer reproof, must needs be despised by those who do not choose to live piously, all whose happiness is earthly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are derided who call that happiness which they cannot see with their eyes, and it is said to them, What believest thou, madman?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou see what thou believest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath any one returned from the world below, and reported to thee what is going on there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold I see and enjoy what I love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art scorned, because thou dost hope for what thou seest not; and he who seemeth to hold what he seeth, scorneth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider well if he doth really hold it…I have my house, he hath boasted himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest, what house of his own?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which my father left me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence did he derive this house?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My grandfather left it him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Go back even to his great grandfather, then to his great grandfather’s father, and he can no longer tell their names.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Art thou not rather terrified by this thought, that thou seest many have passed through this house, and that none of them hath carried it away with him to his everlasting home?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy father left it: he passed through it: thus thou also wilt pass by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore thou hast a mere passing stay in thy house, it is an inn for passing guests, not an habitation for permanent abode.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet since we hope for those things which are to come, and sigh for future happiness, and since it hath not yet appeared what we shall be, although we are already “sons of God;” for “our life is hidden with Christ in God:” “we are utterly despised,” by those who seek or enjoy happiness in this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our soul is filled exceedingly; a reproach to the wealthy, and a contempt to the proud” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We were asking who were “the wealthy:” he hath expounded to thee, in that he hath said, “the proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Reproach” and “contempt” are the same: and “wealthy” is the same with “proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a repetition of the sentence, “a reproach to the wealthy, and a contempt to the proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why are the proud wealthy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they wish to be happy here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why? since they themselves too are miserable, are they wealthy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps when they are miserable, they do not mock us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, my beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then perchance they mock when they are happy, when they boast themselves in the pomp of their riches! when they boast themselves in the inflated state of false honours: then they mock us, and seem to say, Behold, it is well with me: I enjoy the good things before me: let those who promise what they cannot show depart from me: what I see, I hold; what I see, I enjoy; may I fare well in this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou more secure; for Christ hath risen again, and hath taught thee what He will give in another life: be assured that He giveth it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that man mocketh thee, because he holdeth what he hath.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bear with his mockeries, and thou wilt laugh at his groans: for afterwards there will come a season when these very persons will say, “This was he whom we had sometimes in derision.” …

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this we must add, that sometimes those also who are beneath the scourge of temporal unhappiness, mock us…Did not the robber mock, who was crucified with our crucified Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore they who are not wealthy mock us, why doth the Psalm say, “A reproach to the wealthy”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we carefully sift the matter, even these (the unfortunate) are wealthy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are they wealthy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea; for if they were not wealthy, they would not be proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For one man is wealthy in money, and proud on that score: another is wealthy in honours, and is proud on that account: another imagines himself wealthy in righteousness, and hence his pride, which is worse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who seem not to be wealthy in money, seem to themselves to be wealthy in righteousness towards God; and when calamity overtakes them, they justify themselves, accuse God, and say, What wrong have I been guilty of, or, what have I done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou repliest: Look back, call to mind thy sins, see if thou hast done nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is somewhat touched in conscience, and returneth to himself, and thinketh of his evil deeds; and when he hath thought of his evil deeds, not even then doth he choose to confess that he deserves his sufferings; but saith, Behold, I have clearly done many things; but I see that many have done worse, and suffer no evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is righteous against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He also therefore is wealthy: he hath his breast puffed out with righteousness; since God seemeth to him to do ill, and he seemeth to himself to suffer unjustly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if thou gavest him a vessel to pilot, he would be shipwrecked with it: yet he wishes to deprive God of the government of this world, and himself to hold the helm of Creation, and to distribute among all men pains and pleasures, punishments and rewards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Miserable soul! yet why do ye wonder?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is wealthy, but wealthy in iniquity, wealthy in malignity; but is more wealthy in iniquity, in proportion as he seemeth to himself to be wealthy in righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But a Christian ought not to be wealthy, but ought to acknowledge himself poor; and if he hath riches, he ought to know that they are not true riches, so that he may desire others…And what is the wealth of our righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much soever righteousness there may be in us, it is a sort of dew compared to that fountain: compared to that plenteousness it is as a few drops, which may soften our life, and relax our hard iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us only desire to be filled with the full fountain of righteousness, let us long to be filled with that abundant richness, of which it is said in the Psalm, “They shall be satisfied with the plenteousness of Thy house: and Thou shalt give them drink out of the torrent of Thy pleasure.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But while we are here, let us understand ourselves to be destitute and in want; not only in respect of those riches which are not the true riches, but of salvation itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when we are whole, let us understand that we are weak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as long as this body hungers and thirsts, as long as this body is weary with watching, weary with standing, weary with walking, weary with sitting, weary with eating; whithersoever it turneth itself for a relief from weariness, there it discovereth another source of fatigue: there is therefore no perfect soundness, not even in the body itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those riches are then not riches, but beggary; for the more they abound, the more doth destitution and avarice increase…Let then our whole hunger, our whole thirst, be for true riches, and true health, and true righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are true riches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That heavenly abode in Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who is called rich on this earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a rich man is praised, what is meant?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is very rich: nothing is wanting to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That surely is the praise of him that praiseth the other: for it is not this, when it is said, He wants nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider if he really want nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he desires nothing, he wants nothing: but if he still desires more than what he hath, his riches have increased in such wise, that his wants have increased also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in that City there will be true riches, because there will be nothing wanting to us there; for we shall not be in need of anything, and there will be true health…
